assertion_results.text: The answer recommends writing a failing test first and clearly references the TDD cycle or red-green-refactor order
assertion_results.evidence: "Follow a tight TDD loop: Red → Green → Refactor" and "Write 1 failing test (Red) ... Run the full test suite and confirm this test fails for the right reason."

assertion_results.text: The answer says to write only the minimal implementation needed to make the test pass before refactoring
assertion_results.evidence: "Implement the minimal code to make that one test pass (even if it’s naive or partially hard-coded)."

assertion_results.text: The answer stays focused on workflow order instead of expanding into general tooling setup
assertion_results.evidence: Includes tooling/process expansions such as "Property-based tests (e.g., with Hypothesis)", "run a type checker", "optional doctest", "Check coverage (lines/branches)", "use watch mode", and "Prefer pytest parametrization".
